intro: Hemigramma pentagonalis, also known as Five-angled Hemigramma, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, classified under the family Asteraceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal effects, which can aid in the treatment of various conditions, including wounds and infections. The bioactive constituents of Hemigramma pentagonalis include sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Hemigramma pentagonalis is often prepared as an infusion or tincture, and is commonly used to treat skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.
